news.title: Fed Keeps Rates Steady at 3.5%-3.75% in April 2026 Meeting
news.desc: The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% at the April 29 FOMC meeting, marking continued pause since January 2026. Markets now eye the June 17 meeting for any signal shift on rate cuts.

news.title: Fed Balance Sheet at $6.7T: Liquidity Remains Historically Supportive
news.desc: Fed total assets at $6,700B as of April 29 — up $25B over 1 month. Bank reserves at $2.90T (down $92B MoM). RRP near $1.63B, TGA at ~$969B. Treasury holdings rose $210B YoY while MBS declined $192B, suggesting a Treasury-centric portfolio shift. Net liquidity remains in the 78th historical percentile.

news.title: Barclays Expects Fed to Hold Rates Through All of 2026
news.desc: Barclays revised its Fed outlook, now forecasting rates remain unchanged through 2026, upgrading from a prior prediction of a 25bp cut in September. The shift reflects persistent inflation and the Fed's cautious stance amid tariff and fiscal uncertainty.
